summary: A pristine matrix recording of the 10/11/83 MSG show captures an electrifying set highlighted by the long‑awaited St. Stephen, soaring Birdsong and a fiery China > Rider.
review: The Silberman digital‑FOB capture is virtually flawless – crisp, well‑balanced and full of just enough audience ambience to convey the massive MSG reaction when St. Stephen finally resurfaced after a decade. Musically the night is a showcase of late‑‘80s Dead energy: an explosive first set opener (Wang Dang >Dawn), a standout Birdsong, a blistering China > Rider transition and a heartfelt China Doll with Phil’s rare mic moment. Overall the performance is consistently strong with only minor vocal raggedness on a few numbers.

ai_rating.rationale: Average reviewer rating is 4.5★, most comments praise both the show and the recording; only occasional minor complaints appear, yielding a strong overall score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Silberman's digital FOB provides crystal‑clear mix with balanced audience ambience. Minor speaker pop during the China > Rider transition is noted, but overall the recording is described as pristine and one of the best audience‑type captures.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A high‑energy first set (Wang Dang >Dawn opener, Jack Straw, Birdsong) leads into a second set that builds momentum with fast China > Rider, a superb Bertha jam and an emotional China Doll, capped by the historic St. Stephen and a lively encore.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for soaring leads, tight solos and even ‘orgasmic’ moments during Birdsong; his voice is noted as slightly ragged in places but still strong.
band_member_comments.Phil: Highlighted for stepping up to the mic on St. Stephen after a 9‑year gap, delivering an unforgettable vocal moment that thrilled the crowd.
band_member_comments.Bob: Mentioned for wide rhythm strokes and solid backing throughout; no major criticisms.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s envelope‑filter jam is called ‘nutty‑crazy’ and adds texture; keyboards are generally praised.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drums > Space sections are described as lively with “skeletons banging” but not singled out for issues.
